user_query:
I'm organizing price comps for my rock memorabilia section and need a clean table of auction results for concert posters. Use the artist list in input/wanted_items.csv as the source of targets. For each artist, use a general-purpose web search engine to find completed/realized auction lot pages from official auction houses only: christies.com (Christie's), sothebys.com (Sotheby's), bonhams.com (Bonhams), and ha.com (Heritage Auctions). Do not use third-party aggregators or marketplaces. Requirements:

1) Discovery and logging
- Perform search engine queries combining the artist name, the term "concert poster", and site: filters for the domains above. Record every query you actually used and the final URLs you chose in logs/search_log.txt.
- Collect at least one completed lot per artist, at least 5 total lots across all artists, and include results from at least two different auction house domains.

2) Download and snapshot
- For every chosen lot page, download the full HTML and save a local snapshot under data/raw/<house>/<id>.html, where <house> is the auction house domain (e.g., ha.com) and <id> is a stable identifier (e.g., a hash of the source URL). Compute and store a SHA-256 of the saved HTML in your outputs.
- If a clear main item image URL is present in the static HTML (no scripting required), download that image and save it under images/<house>/<id>.<ext> (preserve the original extension). If not available, leave the image field blank. All extraction should be performed from the downloaded HTML files, not from live pages in memory.
- If a page requires client-side rendering to reveal details, skip it and choose a different lot page.

3) Data extraction and table
- Produce a CSV at data/comps.csv with one row per lot and the following columns (exact names): house, source_url, local_html_path, page_sha256, artist, item_type, lot_title, sale_date_iso, realized_price_value, realized_price_currency, image_path, matched_keywords, extracted_at_utc.
- Populate fields as follows:
  • house: the auction house domain (one of christies.com, sothebys.com, bonhams.com, ha.com).
  • source_url: the original lot page URL used for the download.
  • local_html_path: relative path to the saved HTML snapshot, e.g., data/raw/ha.com/<id>.html.
  • page_sha256: SHA-256 of the saved HTML file.
  • artist: the artist name from the input file that this lot is associated with.
  • item_type: set to concert poster.
  • lot_title: the lot title from the page.
  • sale_date_iso: the sale date in YYYY-MM-DD if available (empty if unknown).
  • realized_price_value: numeric realized price (numbers only; no commas or symbols). Leave empty if the page only shows estimates and no realized price.
  • realized_price_currency: currency code or symbol as shown (e.g., USD, GBP, $, £) if realized price is present; empty otherwise.
  • image_path: relative path to the downloaded image if any.
  • matched_keywords: pipe-delimited subset of era_keywords from the input that you found in the lot title or description (case-insensitive). If none, leave empty.
  • extracted_at_utc: UTC timestamp of extraction in ISO 8601 format.

4) Provenance
- Create data/provenance.json that, for each row, records: source_url, local_html_path, house, artist, search_query_used, and the local image path if one was saved. Use an array of objects.

Notes and constraints:
- Only include completed/realized lot pages from the domains specified above. If the realized price is not shown on a page, you may include the lot if the sale date is present, but make sure the realized price fields are left empty. Do not include upcoming lots or estimate-only catalog pages without a sale date.
- All referenced local files (HTML and images) must exist and paths in data/comps.csv must be valid.
- The task is complete when data/comps.csv has ≥5 rows total, covers all artists from input/wanted_items.csv with ≥1 row per artist, includes at least two distinct auction house domains, logs/search_log.txt lists the queries and chosen URLs, and data/provenance.json matches the rows and local files.

Deliverables to produce under the workspace:
- data/comps.csv
- data/provenance.json
- data/raw/<house>/*.html (one per lot)
- images/<house>/* (if any images were downloaded)
- logs/search_log.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
